purpose: Define a source-safe release/readiness snapshot that aggregates preview validators, gates, prototype audit status and Phase A operational readiness before any real activation, deploy, merge, import or provider connection.
readinessRules: Release readiness snapshot is preview only and cannot trigger deploy, merge, import or writes.
Every readiness item must state its area, validator, status, gate and blocking reason when blocked.
Any real activation request must remain blocked until explicit future approval and production plan exist.
Sensitive data policy must be preview-ready or approved preview before any source input is considered.
Prototype audit must be aligned with latest audited baseline before release readiness is reported.
Readiness can be approved preview-only even when production gates stay off.
Missing sanitized input blocks validator execution but does not block continued document/backend contract work.
Academia readiness must be tracked as a first-class area, not an afterthought.
Any conflict in assignment, lifecycle, payments, communication or rules forces manual_review_required or blocked_conflict.
claudePrototypeImpact: Prototype should eventually show readiness/gate status honestly: preview ready, pending backend, missing input, review required or blocked.
Do not show production ready, connected, deployed, sent, synced or imported unless future gates are explicitly active.
Academia must explain release readiness, gates and why preview-ready is not production-ready.
Readiness dashboard should separate prototype pending from backend pending and data/source pending.

newLessons: How missing input differs from a defect
interactiveChecklists: Before marking a release area ready
Before telling users a feature is connected

hardStops: Do not deploy from this snapshot.
Do not merge from this snapshot.
Do not import real data from this snapshot.
Do not write Firestore, Storage, HR or providers from this snapshot.
Do not send email, WhatsApp or Make from this snapshot.
Do not call Gemini from this snapshot.
Do not mark production ready while any real gate remains off or approval is missing.
